        (try simp_all) <;>
        (try tauto)
        <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₃ := T_ext x y
            simp_all [h₂]
            <;>
            (try tauto)
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₃ := T_ext x y
            simp_all [h₂]
            <;>
            (try tauto)
          })
      have h₃ : ∀ (u v : U), u = v := by
        intro u v
        by_cases h : u = v <;> simp_all
        <;>
        (try contradiction) <;>
        (try exfalso) <;>
        (try simp_all) <;>
        (try tauto)
        <;>
        (try
          {
            have h₄ := T_ext x y
            simp_all [h₂]
            <;>
            (try tauto)
          })
      have h₄ : x = y := by
        have h₅ : u = v := by tauto
        have h₆ : ∀ (u v : U), u = v := by tauto
        have h₇ : x = y := by
          -- Use the `T_ext` axiom to conclude `x = y` given `u = v` and `∀ (u v : U), u = v`.
          have h₈ : x = y := h₁ h₅ h₆
          exact h₈
        exact h₇
      -- Use the conclusion `x = y` to close the proof.
      exact h₄
